howItWorks.intro: This diesel fuel cost per mile calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Cost per mile = diesel price / MPG
Daily cost = cost per mile × daily miles
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What's the average MPG for a semi truck?
faq.answer: A loaded Class 8 semi truck averages 5.5-6.5 MPG. Empty trucks can achieve 7-8 MPG. Newer aerodynamic models may reach 8-10 MPG.

faq.question: How does diesel price affect shipping costs?
faq.answer: Fuel is typically 30-40% of trucking operating costs. A $1/gallon increase in diesel adds roughly $0.15 per mile, or about $70-80 per day for a long-haul trucker.
